short_description: Price mismatch observed between SRP and HDP for multiple hotels and rate plans
close_notes:
Issue Summary: Price mismatch observed between SRP and HDP for multiple hotels and rate plans on 30TH APRIL 
Solution Provided: We noticed that there was price mismatch between SRP and HDP for the hotel ROCTHY, SLOWES, LONCIT, WELSTA . AvCache DB was not being updated with the latest updates and hence SRP prices were showing some mismatches. Further OnDemand job was run for the hotel (ROCTHY, SLOWES, LONCIT, WELSTA) for 375days from today. Post which, the latest Opera prices (HDP) got updated to the AvCache DB prices (SRP). This resolved the price discrepancies.
Related Tickets: INC0541949

short_description: [P-26041091] [Problem] Marketing Access Token Error  from 08:30?AM to 08:42 AM BST
close_notes:
Issue Summary : [P-26041091]  [Problem] Marketing Access Token Error from 08:30?AM to 08:42 AM BST, 29th Apr 2026.

Solution Provided : We noticed increase in errors on marketing service between 08:30?AM to 08:42 AM BST, 29th Apr 2026. The errors were due to "CDH Exception - Unauthorized. Access token is missing or invalid." with a 401 response. As an agreed workaround, the faulty token was evicted from the Redis cache to mitigate the issue. Post which the errors stopped and the service failure rate was back to normal. No further actions required. CTECH-3878 has been raised for the fix from Engg team. Hence resolving the incident.

Related Tickets : CTECH-3878

short_description: PIB SRP and HDP were very slow to load, with the spinner continuing to spin indefinitely  during the testing of an hotfix
close_notes:
Issue Summary : PIB SRP and HDP were slow to load around 6:30AM 27th Apr, with the spinner continuing to spin indefinitely across different browsers as well as incognito mode.

Solution Provided : During the issue window, we analyzed the calls made to PIB SRP and PIB HDP and observed that most requests to Opera for fetching rate information, availability, and minimum rate availability were responding slower (~ 4-5secs) than usual, which led to increased load times. The issue was intermittent and persisted for approximately 15–20 minutes. Subsequently, PIB SRP and HDP began loading without latency, as confirmed by QA. Further no such issues noticed.  Hence resolving the incident. 

Related tickets : NA

short_description: Guest Support CC - CCUI
close_notes:
Resolution notes copied from Parent Incident: Hi Team,

Around the time of the recent CDH production release, CCUI users reported multiple issues, including application downtime and generic error responses such as "Something went wrong. Please try again later."

As a precaution, the incident was analyzed for any potential correlation with the CDH production deployment.

A detailed investigation was carried out by the Digital BAU team, involving:

Review of CCUI application error logs.
Analysis of downstream dependency and API call traces.
Validation of CDH API request/response patterns during the reported impact window.


Log?level :

No errors, exceptions, or elevated failure rates were observed from CDH APIs.
No timeout, connectivity, authentication, or authorization issues were detected on the CDH side.
There is no log correlation indicating that the CCUI errors were triggered by CDH APIs or the recent CDH deployment.


Next Steps :

Kindly reassign this ticket to the Digital BAU team, who will continue the investigation in coordination with Karolina and team to identify and resolve the root cause within the CCUI layer.

Closure from Avanade Side:

As there is no impact identified from the CDH end and CDH services are operating as expected, this ticket is being closed from the Avanade/CDH end.

Regards,
Akanksha

short_description: Employee booking failed yesterday (19Apr2026)
close_notes:
Booking basket expired due to user inactivity exceeding the 30-minute basket validity window before payment completion.

The user initiated a booking at 11:30 AM (GMT), but there was no activity for approximately 25 minutes. The payment process began at 11:57 AM, and the payment ID 71873232243D was successfully created at 12:00:57 PM.
However, the booking basket expired before payment completion. As per system design, the basket remains active only for 30 minutes. Due to this timeout, the payment could not be linked to an active basket, resulting in booking failure.
Subsequently, as per Planet Portal, the user re-attempted the booking using the employee rate, and the booking was successfully confirmed in Opera.

short_description: [P-2604594] [Problem] AEM Content Replication error from 01:24?PM to 01:50 PM BST
close_notes:
Issue:

->Replication in AEM was blocked because one of the publisher agents had an incorrect configuration. Specifically, the replication agent's URL/IP address was missing, which prevented successful communication with the publisher.

Impact:

->Pages replicated from Author were not delivered to Publisher. The replication queue was blocked, resulting in pending items that could not be processed until the issue was resolved.

Workaround Applied:

->Logged into the Author instance.

->Navigated to the replication agent's configuration.

-> Identified the publisher agent with missing IP address in the replicating URL.

->Updated the agent configuration with the correct publisher IP to restore replication.

Checks Performed:

->Verified replication agent status in Author.

->Confirmed that the missing IP was the root cause of the blocked queue.

->Ensured replication resumed successfully after updating the IP.

->An infra ticket for this incident https://whitbreadis.atlassian.net/browse/INFRA-1514

short_description: Few pages failing for header
close_notes:
Issue:

---> Due to dispatcher cache, the header was not picked up correctly. The Next.js application continued to load the old image endpoint instead of the updated one.

Impact:

---> When opening the Terms & Conditions page, the header was missing. In the browser's network tab, the Next.js endpoint showed the old image reference.

Root Cause:

--->The old image reference was still present in the dispatcher cache. Since the cache had not expired, the new image endpoint was not being served.

Workaround Applied:

---> Verified the issue by opening the Terms & Conditions page and checking the network tab (old image reference was still loading).

---> Navigated to the dispatcher cache path.
cd /opt/aem/dispatcher/httpd_www/aem_site/content/pi/websites/desktop/gb/en/unsecured/terms

---> Used grep to identify pages still referencing the old image.
grep -rnw "69987e2af36a561f.js"

---> Cleared the cache for the affected pages using the dispatcher command:
rm -rf <affected page ex: disabled-access.html>

---> Cleared cache across all dispatchers (whether pollex or cluster), ensuring the currently live dispatcher was fully refreshed.

---> After clearing, refreshed the page and confirmed the header was picked up correctly with the new image reference for both UK and German pages.

---> An infra ticket has been raised, https://whitbreadis.atlassian.net/browse/CTECH-7987

short_description: [P-2604130]  Failure rate increased in hotel-reservation-entity-service:ManageBookingController from 12:45? AM to 01:21 AM BST
close_notes:
As verified, the invalid cancellation requests are originating from the distribution channel (Travelport),PI.com, Mobile app. The booking arrival date was 3 April, while partners/users attempted to cancel the booking in the early hours of 4 April, resulting in multiple retry attempts for the cancellation request. Additionally, upon verifying a few affected bookings in the OPERA system, the status is confirmed as AYO7348814-No?Show. There are also multiple room bookings (AYO7348814) where users were unable to check in on 3 April as per the booking arrival date; however, records show that the users checked in on 4 April.

short_description: [P-260449]  Failure rate increase  in  liveres.co.uk from 12:01?AM  to 01:04 AM
close_notes:
Most of the Restaurant failure alerts are caused by users searching for slots on past dates.

Exception: GraphQLError
Message:
{"message":"Bad Request","errors":[{"field":"Error","message":"Date cannot be in the past."}],"statusCode":400}

Affected URL : https://api.liveres.co.uk/events/v1/slots?from=2026-04-01&until=2026-04-01&adults=2&children=0&siteId=b3407000-6423-4e25-99e2-03d3a5b91c1b

Some failures are also due to getLocations errors where the connection closed early (400 Bad Request). During the affected window, the AEM endpoint
https://www.tabletable.co.uk/en-gb/locations.search.json was not loading. As a result, restaurant IDs were not generated, causing the getLocations API to fail. Since getLocations did not return a valid location details, no downstream calls were triggered to Zonal. This indicates an issue with the AEM service during that period.

close_notes:

short_description: [P-26031084]  Failure rate increased in opera-apollo-subgraphs:graphql.execute from 02:45 PM to 03:47 PM BST
close_notes:
Root cause:
The issue occurred because no amendments were being attempted by a user on a BUSIFLEX reservation after the deadline, leading to a failure rate increase in the opera-apollo-subgraphs:graphql.execute service due to 'RuleEngineException' when the rule was empty for amendment requests. 

Resolution steps:
- Identified that the failure rate increase was caused by attempts to process amendment requests for BUSIFLEX reservations after the deadline, resulting in empty rules for the AmendmentRuleController.

short_description: BIC on CCUI and PI.com showing incorrect room type when booking a ZPLDBL as a tw
close_notes: The search was performed for a Twin Room, but no twin rooms were available at that time. As a result, the booking was silently substituted with ZPLDBL, with TW2S (two single beds) added as special preferences. The booking was created with a room using the ZPLDBL room type. As per the current AEM configuration, the ZPLDBL roomTypeCode is labeled as "Double Room." Based on OPERA availability and the silent substitution rule on 3rd March, ZPLDBL was configured for the booking. This behavior is working as expected as per the current design.

short_description: DE - Digital - CCUI - We cannot see any address details which are required for DPA Check in CCUI on this booking GCA7299422
close_notes:
Issue Summary :  We cannot see any address details which are required for DPA Check in CCUI on this booking GCA7299422.

Solution Provided : The issue is caused by the address object being missing in the update reservation API call. The development team has already been informed and is working on it under ticket CTECH-6215. As a workaround, we updated booking GCA7299422 with the company address as confirmed by the caller over Teams chat, and the address is now visible in the DPA check-in modal. Further updates will be provided as part of CTECH-6215. The caller has also confirmed closure of the incident, and the incident is therefore resolved

Related tickets : INC0570406/ CTECH-6215

short_description: [DNRQ-85308] Kiosk - Getting Invalid Session Id at Checkin stage
close_notes:
Issue Summary : Kiosk - Getting Invalid Session Id at Checkin stage

Solution Provided :  Upon reviewing the requests, we can confirm that the issue is not on our side. It appears to originate from Evoke, where they attempt to trigger the CompleteCheckIn request a second time after a successful check-in. There is already a problem ticket (PRB0040612) created for this issue, with parent incident INC0485437.Therefore no further investigation is required on this and hence we are resolving the incident.

Related Tickets : PRB0040612/ INC0485437
